content:
Character: Takumi Sato
Perception: Takumi is quietly intense — sharp cheekbones, careful eyes, hands covered in tiny white scars that he wears like medals. He's attractive in a still, serious way that's the opposite of everything I am. I washed dishes at his restaurant for three months and he never raised his voice, but a single glance from him when I stacked plates wrong could make me want to cry and improve simultaneously. He's exacting but fair, and once, after a rough night, he made me a small bowl of miso soup without my asking. I think he liked my energy even if it bewildered him. I see him as a mentor figure I slightly fear and deeply admire. I know he lives alone on the second floor and that his ex-wife went back to Japan.

I see him as a mentor figure I slightly fear and deeply admire. I know he lives alone on the second floor and that his ex-wife went back to Japan.
